framework.name: NCSC Cyber Essentials Plus
framework.scope: Hands-on technical verification of Cyber Essentials controls by an accredited assessor, including vulnerability scanning and configuration testing
framework.structure_description: 5 technical control areas verified through hands-on testing: external vulnerability scan, internal build review, multi-factor authentication test, account separation test, and malware protection test

controls.title: External vulnerability assessment
controls.description: An accredited assessor performs an external vulnerability scan of all internet-facing IP addresses and hostnames provided by the applicant. The scan must identify no critical or high-severity vulnerabilities that have patches or fixes available.
controls.description_nl: An accredited assessor performs an external vulnerability scan of all internet-facing IP addresses and hostnames provided by the applicant. The scan must identify no critical or high-severity vulnerabilities that have patches or fixes available.
controls.implementation_guidance: Ensure all internet-facing systems are patched and properly configured before the assessment. Remediate all critical and high-severity vulnerabilities. Conduct pre-assessment scanning internally to identify issues.
controls.verification_guidance: External vulnerability scan of all in-scope IP addresses and hostnames. No critical or high-severity patchable vulnerabilities allowed.

controls.title: Internet-facing services hardened
controls.description: All internet-facing services must be identified, documented, and hardened. Unnecessary services must be disabled. Remaining services must run on supported software versions with current patches applied.
controls.description_nl: All internet-facing services must be identified, documented, and hardened. Unnecessary services must be disabled. Remaining services must run on supported software versions with current patches applied.
controls.implementation_guidance: Inventory all internet-facing services. Disable unnecessary ports and protocols. Verify all running software is supported and patched.
controls.verification_guidance: Port scan and service enumeration of external IP addresses. Verify only documented services are accessible.

controls.title: TLS configuration verified
controls.description: All internet-facing services using TLS must be configured with TLS 1.2 or later. Weak cipher suites and deprecated protocol versions must be disabled.
controls.description_nl: All internet-facing services using TLS must be configured with TLS 1.2 or later. Weak cipher suites and deprecated protocol versions must be disabled.
controls.implementation_guidance: Configure all internet-facing services to require TLS 1.2 or later. Disable SSLv2, SSLv3, TLS 1.0, and TLS 1.1. Remove weak cipher suites.
controls.verification_guidance: TLS configuration scan of all HTTPS endpoints. Verify no legacy protocols or weak ciphers are offered.

controls.title: Internal build review
controls.description: The assessor samples a representative set of end-user devices and servers to verify they meet Cyber Essentials secure configuration requirements. This includes checking patch levels, configuration hardening, and unnecessary software removal.
controls.description_nl: The assessor samples a representative set of end-user devices and servers to verify they meet Cyber Essentials secure configuration requirements. This includes checking patch levels, configuration hardening, and unnecessary software removal.
controls.implementation_guidance: Apply standard build images to all devices. Verify all sampled devices are fully patched, have unnecessary software removed, and meet hardening baselines. Include Windows, macOS, and Linux devices in scope.
controls.verification_guidance: Hands-on examination of sampled devices for patch levels, installed software, running services, and configuration settings.

controls.title: Patch levels verified on sampled devices
controls.title_nl: Patch levels verified on sampled devices
controls.description: All sampled end-user devices and servers must have critical and high-risk security patches applied within 14 days of release. Operating systems and applications must be on supported versions.
controls.description_nl: All sampled end-user devices and servers must have critical and high-risk security patches applied within 14 days of release. Operating systems and applications must be on supported versions.
controls.implementation_guidance: Implement automated patch management. Verify patch status on all device types before assessment. Remove end-of-life software.
controls.verification_guidance: Check installed patch levels against vendor bulletins. Verify no critical or high-risk patches are outstanding beyond 14 days.

controls.title: Secure configuration verified on sampled devices
controls.title_nl: Secure configuration verified on sampled devices
controls.description: Sampled devices must demonstrate secure configuration: default passwords changed, unnecessary software removed, auto-run disabled, host-based firewall enabled, screen lock configured.
controls.description_nl: Sampled devices must demonstrate secure configuration: default passwords changed, unnecessary software removed, auto-run disabled, host-based firewall enabled, screen lock configured.
controls.implementation_guidance: Apply hardening baselines to all devices. Verify screen lock is set to 15 minutes or less. Confirm host firewall is active. Remove bloatware and unnecessary applications.
controls.verification_guidance: Hands-on check of device configuration against Cyber Essentials requirements: firewall status, screen lock timeout, installed applications, auto-run setting.

controls.title: Multi-factor authentication test
controls.description: The assessor verifies that multi-factor authentication (MFA) is configured and enforced for all cloud services and remote access where available. MFA must use at least two of: something you know, something you have, something you are.
controls.description_nl: The assessor verifies that multi-factor authentication (MFA) is configured and enforced for all cloud services and remote access where available. MFA must use at least two of: something you know, something you have, something you are.
controls.implementation_guidance: Enable MFA on all cloud services (Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, AWS, etc.). Apply MFA for VPN and remote desktop access. Use authenticator apps or hardware tokens rather than SMS where possible.
controls.verification_guidance: Attempt login to cloud services without MFA to verify enforcement. Test MFA flow on sampled services.

controls.title: Cloud service MFA enforced
controls.description: All cloud services accessible to organisational users must have MFA enforced. This includes email, file storage, CRM, and any SaaS applications where the provider supports MFA.
controls.description_nl: All cloud services accessible to organisational users must have MFA enforced. This includes email, file storage, CRM, and any SaaS applications where the provider supports MFA.
controls.implementation_guidance: Audit all cloud services for MFA capability. Enable and enforce MFA on every service that supports it. Document any services that do not support MFA and apply compensating controls.
controls.verification_guidance: Test login to sampled cloud services to confirm MFA is required. Review admin console MFA enforcement settings.

controls.title: Account separation test
controls.description: The assessor verifies that administrative accounts are separate from standard user accounts and that administrative privileges are not used for day-to-day activities such as email and web browsing.
controls.description_nl: The assessor verifies that administrative accounts are separate from standard user accounts and that administrative privileges are not used for day-to-day activities such as email and web browsing.
controls.implementation_guidance: Create separate admin accounts for all administrators. Ensure admin accounts are not used for email, web browsing, or other routine tasks. Apply the principle of least privilege.
controls.verification_guidance: Review account lists for separation of admin and user accounts. Verify admin accounts are not assigned mailboxes or internet access.

controls.title: Least privilege verified
controls.description: User accounts must be configured with the minimum privileges needed for their role. No standard user accounts should have local administrator rights unless there is a documented, approved business justification.
controls.description_nl: User accounts must be configured with the minimum privileges needed for their role. No standard user accounts should have local administrator rights unless there is a documented, approved business justification.
controls.implementation_guidance: Remove local administrator rights from standard user accounts. Document and approve any exceptions. Implement privileged access management where applicable.
controls.verification_guidance: Check sampled user accounts for local admin rights. Review exception documentation.

controls.title: Malware protection test
controls.description: The assessor verifies that anti-malware software is installed, active, and up to date on all sampled devices. The assessor tests whether known malware samples are detected and blocked.
controls.description_nl: The assessor verifies that anti-malware software is installed, active, and up to date on all sampled devices. The assessor tests whether known malware samples are detected and blocked.
controls.implementation_guidance: Deploy anti-malware on all endpoints. Verify automatic updates are functioning. Test detection using EICAR test files. Ensure real-time protection is enabled.
controls.verification_guidance: Verify anti-malware is installed and active on sampled devices. Test detection with EICAR test file. Check signature currency.

controls.title: Anti-malware signatures current
controls.description: Anti-malware signature definitions must be updated at least daily. Automatic updates must be enabled and verified to be functioning.
controls.description_nl: Anti-malware signature definitions must be updated at least daily. Automatic updates must be enabled and verified to be functioning.
controls.implementation_guidance: Enable automatic signature updates. Verify update mechanism is working. Monitor for devices with outdated signatures.
controls.verification_guidance: Check signature dates on sampled devices. Verify automatic updates are configured and recent.

controls.title: Malware download prevention verified
controls.description: The assessor tests that the organisation can prevent execution of known malware by attempting to download test malware files (EICAR) through the web browser and verifying they are blocked.
controls.description_nl: The assessor tests that the organisation can prevent execution of known malware by attempting to download test malware files (EICAR) through the web browser and verifying they are blocked.
controls.implementation_guidance: Ensure web filtering or endpoint protection blocks known malware downloads. Test with EICAR test files in various formats (plain, zip, double-zip).
controls.verification_guidance: Attempt download of EICAR test files through web browser. Verify detection and blocking.
